reasoning: On July 31, 2025, at the World Aquatics Championships, Summer McIntosh won the 200m butterfly with a time of 2:01.99, missing Liu Zige's 2009 supersuit world record of 2:01.81 by just 0.18 seconds. Summer was in peak physical taper for this race and did not break the record. The long-course swimming season traditionally ends after the summer World Championships. Between September and December, elite swimmers primarily focus on short-course (25m) competitions, culminating in the Short Course World Championships. There are virtually no major long-course meets where a swimmer would be fully tapered to take down one of the toughest records in the sport before late spring 2026. Consequently, the likelihood of this 16-year-old long-course world record falling before January 30, 2026, is very low. My probabilities start at the maximum allowed (0.95) for the nearest date and decay slightly as the horizon increases, accounting for the remote possibility of an unexpected long-course record attempt.
